name: Steepleaf
mission: The herbarium cabinet — botanical plates, habitat keys, and phenology through the year. Botany taught the way a good field guide teaches: illustration, range, and respect for rare plants.
learnToday: How to read a botanical plate: leaf arrangement, margin, venation, and habitat
Pike County spring phenology: what is leafing, flowering, and fruiting this week
Ethics of wild plant observation — photograph, don't poach rare species
Connecting understory plants to canopy trees on north vs south slopes
comingSoon: Species plates with illustrations and range maps
Phenology calendar for Northeast PA natives
Habitat keys: wetland margin, dry ridge, ravine, old field
Conservation alerts for sensitive species — observe only

preview.title: The herbarium page
preview.description: Steepleaf opens like a cabinet drawer — each species on a plate you can study before you walk. Learn the leaf, then find it with your eyes. Rare plants stay on the page; the field is for observation, not collection.
